provenance: Synthetic fixtures authored for the JBM taste evaluation on 2026-07-15.

briefs.title: An essay with inspectable sources
briefs.audience: Curious readers arriving from a newsletter
briefs.goal: Help a reader understand the essay's argument, read in a comfortable sequence, and inspect its two cited sources.
requiredContent: Title: The Sidewalk Is a Small Archive
Dek: What curb cuts, chalk marks, and repaired cracks remember about a block
Three sections titled Marks, Repairs, and Return
A source note for the city curb-ramp inventory and a 2025 neighborhood walking interview
facts: Marks argues that temporary marks reveal how a block is used before official records catch up.
Repairs compares three visible patch types: asphalt seam, concrete square, and painted utility cut.
Return concludes that maintenance is a form of public memory when its provenance stays visible.
City curb-ramp inventory source URL: https://example.org/fixtures/curb-ramps
Walking interview source URL: https://example.org/fixtures/walking-interview
Provenance: all people, text, and URLs in this brief are synthetic evaluation fixtures.
constraints: Do not invent reading progress, quotations, footnotes, or source metadata.
Keep long-form reading comfortable at narrow and wide widths.

briefs.title: A command-line recovery guide
briefs.audience: A developer whose local validation stopped before making changes
briefs.goal: Explain what failed, show the shortest safe recovery sequence, and distinguish checks from mutations.
requiredContent: Error: Node 22.22.0 does not match required 22.23.1
A clearly labeled non-destructive diagnosis command
facts: Step 1: install Node 22.23.1 with the reader's existing version manager.
Step 2: run npm ci from the repository root.
Step 3: run npm run doctor, then npm run verify.
Diagnosis command: node --version
Healthy signal: bootstrap complete; run npm run verify
No files were changed before the version check stopped the original command.
requiredActions: Follow the recovery steps in order
constraints: Do not add fake terminal output, timing, progress, or success state.
Commands must remain readable without JavaScript.

briefs.title: A volunteer shift signup
briefs.audience: Neighbors choosing one Saturday garden shift
briefs.goal: Make remaining capacity comparable and help a volunteer choose and request one suitable shift.
requiredContent: Four shifts with time, task, accessibility note, capacity, and remaining spaces
facts: 08:00–09:30, Tool setup, paved route, capacity 4, 1 space remaining.
09:30–11:00, Seedling beds, uneven soil, capacity 8, 5 spaces remaining.
11:00–12:30, Lunch service, seated task available, capacity 6, 2 spaces remaining.
12:30–14:00, Tool cleanup, paved route, capacity 4, 4 spaces remaining.
Volunteers request a shift by emailing garden@example.org; the fixture has no live booking backend.
Provenance: the event and availability are synthetic evaluation data.
requiredActions: Start an email requesting one shift
constraints: Do not present the email action as a confirmed reservation.
Capacity and accessibility cannot rely on color alone.

briefs.title: A tidepool observation record
briefs.goal: Make a small field dataset understandable without turning it into scientific-looking decoration.
requiredContent: Two named sites and four species counts
A textual conclusion supported by the supplied counts
facts: North Shelf counts: ochre sea star 3, aggregating anemone 18, hermit crab 11, lined shore crab 2.
South Pocket counts: ochre sea star 1, aggregating anemone 26, hermit crab 7, lined shore crab 5.
Observation window: 07:10–07:40 local time.
Tide: 0.4 feet at 07:22; weather: overcast, 14°C, light wind.
Supported conclusion: South Pocket had more anemones and shore crabs; North Shelf had more sea stars and hermit crabs.
Provenance: synthetic field observations for interface evaluation, not scientific evidence.
constraints: Every plotted mark, tick, legend, and label must derive from supplied data.
Provide a complete textual equivalent for any chart or diagram.

briefs.title: A small public-domain catalog
briefs.audience: A reader browsing downloadable books without an account
briefs.goal: Help a reader scan six records, distinguish formats and languages, and open a download source.
requiredContent: Six book records with author, year, language, formats, and source URL
A visible statement that all records are public-domain fixtures
facts: Pride and Prejudice — Jane Austen — 1813 — English — EPUB and HTML — https://www.gutenberg.org/ebooks/1342
Narrative of the Life of Frederick Douglass — Frederick Douglass — 1845 — English — EPUB and HTML — https://www.gutenberg.org/ebooks/23
The Yellow Wallpaper — Charlotte Perkins Gilman — 1892 — English — EPUB and HTML — https://www.gutenberg.org/ebooks/1952
Siddhartha — Hermann Hesse — 1922 — German — EPUB and HTML — https://www.gutenberg.org/ebooks/2499
Cuentos de Amor de Locura y de Muerte — Horacio Quiroga — 1917 — Spanish — EPUB and HTML — https://www.gutenberg.org/ebooks/13507
The Souls of Black Folk — W. E. B. Du Bois — 1903 — English — EPUB and HTML — https://www.gutenberg.org/ebooks/408
constraints: Filtering may enhance the page but all records must remain available without JavaScript.
Do not fabricate ratings, covers, availability, or download counts.

briefs.title: An urgent transit detour
briefs.audience: Riders deciding whether their evening trip is affected
briefs.goal: Communicate current impact, affected stops, replacement stops, and the next update without requiring a map.
requiredContent: Effective: 2026-07-15 from 18:00 to end of service
Two closed stops and two replacement stops
Next update time and rider contact
facts: Closed inbound stops: Pine at 4th; Pine at 7th.
Replacement inbound stops: Oak at 4th; Oak at 8th.
Cause: permitted night market street closure.
Trips before 18:00 use the normal route.
Next update: 2026-07-15 at 21:00.
Rider contact: 555-0108; all route and contact data are synthetic fixtures.
requiredActions: Determine whether a trip is affected
constraints: Current status and stop changes cannot rely on motion or color alone.
Do not invent a live vehicle map, countdown, or service restoration estimate.

briefs.title: A software release ledger
briefs.audience: Operators checking what changed before upgrading
briefs.goal: Make three releases comparable and distinguish changes, fixes, and required operator action.
requiredContent: Change type and operator action for every entry
A source commit for every version
facts: 2.4.0 — 2026-07-10 — change: adds resumable exports — operator action: review new 24-hour export retention — commit a13f6de.
2.3.1 — 2026-06-28 — fix: prevents duplicate retry notices — operator action: none — commit 09bc221.
2.3.0 — 2026-06-14 — change: adds per-folder sync pause — operator action: verify paused-folder policy — commit e77d90a.
Version numbers, dates, commits, and product are synthetic evaluation fixtures.
Newest supported version in this fixture: 2.4.0.
constraints: Do not fabricate severity, adoption, support, or deployment status.
Chronology and change type must be understandable without color.

briefs.title: An oral-history listening archive
briefs.audience: Families and researchers choosing an interview to hear or read
briefs.goal: Present three interviews with respectful context, duration, topics, transcript status, and rights notes.
requiredContent: Duration, recording date, topics, transcript status, and rights note for each
facts: Marta Ruiz — recorded 1988-04-12 — 42 minutes — topics: bakery, migration, night shift — transcript complete — family-approved educational use.
Theo Green — recorded 1991-09-03 — 28 minutes — topics: bus depot, jazz club, block association — transcript complete — family-approved educational use.
Aiko Tan — recorded 1994-02-19 — 51 minutes — topics: garment work, school garden, tenant organizing — transcript excerpt only — reading-room use.
Audio is not included in the fixture; listening controls must not imply playable media.
Provenance: all interviews and rights statements are synthetic evaluation fixtures.
requiredActions: Read available transcript information and rights context
constraints: Do not invent portraits, quotations, audio waveforms, or playable controls.
Rights and transcript availability must remain adjacent to each record.

briefs.title: A timed community workshop agenda
briefs.audience: Participants preparing for and following a ninety-minute workshop
briefs.goal: Orient participants before arrival and make the live sequence, materials, and accessibility support easy to follow.
requiredContent: Five timed agenda items totaling ninety minutes
Materials, accessibility support, and facilitator contact
facts: 2026-08-20, 18:00–19:30, Cedar Room at North Library.
18:00 welcome and access check — 10 minutes.
18:10 mark difficult crossings — 20 minutes.
18:30 compare routes in small groups — 25 minutes.
18:55 choose two follow-up walks — 20 minutes; 19:15 commitments and close — 15 minutes.
Materials provided: printed map, thick markers, tactile dots. Step-free entrance and live captions available. Contact access@example.org.
constraints: Do not add registration, attendance, or live progress state.
The sequence must remain usable in print and without JavaScript.

briefs.title: A repairable radio comparison
briefs.audience: A buyer comparing three fictional radios for emergency use
briefs.goal: Support an honest comparison of price, power, repairability, warranty, and supplied trade-offs without manufacturing a winner.
requiredContent: Price, power sources, replaceable parts, warranty, and weight for each
A supplied trade-off note for each model
facts: Field 1 — $42 — AA batteries and USB-C — replaceable antenna and battery contacts — 1-year warranty — 410 g — lightest, no hand crank.
Harbor 2 — $68 — internal battery, USB-C, and hand crank — replaceable battery pack and crank — 2-year warranty — 690 g — most power options, heaviest.
Mesa AM/FM — $55 — AA batteries and solar trickle charge — replaceable speaker and tuning dial — 1-year warranty — 530 g — replaceable speaker, slow solar charge.
All products, prices, specifications, and warranties are synthetic evaluation fixtures dated 2026-07-01.
No durability test, reception test, or editorial ranking was supplied.
requiredActions: Choose based on a reader's own priorities
constraints: Do not add ratings, badges, test results, inventory, discounts, or a recommended winner.
The comparison must reflow without hiding attributes at narrow widths.
